title: Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit
notes: Setting shadows off makes it 46-50FPS. taskset -pc 1-7 <pid> makes it use all cores (but doesn't have any noticeable affect on performance)

title: Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit
notes: Just hanging on the Steam Big picture screen, no splash screen. After ca. 10 minutes I restartet steam.

title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit
notes: Worked flawlessly, did not try multiplayer but connecting to the servers appears to work. Note that the colors in the rear view mirror are intentional for contrast, it is intentional by design. I could not replicate any problems alt+tabbing between windows.

title: Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit
notes: Have not tried online features.

title: Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit
notes: Performance not great (could play it fine on Windows). With multi-monitors, focusing off it causes it to hang will stay like that even after focusing again (in i3wm). (Even with shift-tabbing first etc. - similar to what it did before with wine).

title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit
notes: It works perfectly out of the box. A launcher asks you about resolution prior to boot

gpu: X.Org AMD Radeon (TM) RX 460 Graphics (POLARIS11 / DRM 3.23.0 / 4.15.0-33-generic, LLVM 5.0.1)
notes: Rub without problem, but 25-40 fps
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: I tested this without even touching the command line. I do have to say that it doesn't run great on my setup (FX 8320, GTX 970). But I'm pretty sure that has to do with the game being almost entirely on one core. I think the game will run pretty well with a more modern CPU :)
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: All game features are fully functional, even online. Only issue is a FPS hit. I get about 45-50fps.
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: Controller not work in menu but do in game!
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: You need disabling esync and the game runs without issues. Use this tweak: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: I drove ~7 races of the career and all the gameplay seems to just work and performance is fine with my setup. Only slight annoyance is that the rear view mirror reflections of opponents are weird. They look to be a single color, whereas reflections of cars from traffic looks fine. Since it has been reported by others, that the game would not shut down, I should mention that I had that problem as well, but upgrading NVIDIA drivers, from 390 to the recommended 396, fixed it for me.
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: Runs flawlessly. No artefacts or stuttering. FPS is decent. Haven't tried it with a controller yet - I've been using keyboard only.
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: everything works fine. but performance is much worse than under windows
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: Install and drive
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: For Fedora 29 disable ESync. Runs Flawlessly after that.
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: Game wouldn't start out of the box but PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 did the trick. Disabling esync like others mentioned also improved performance, so I'm using: PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: fps reasonable (45 ~ 55), I tried the WINED3D but it gave in the same, with ESYNC the fps drops to 30
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: Funciona tudo perfeitamente. Inclusive o controle do xbox 360.
title: Need For Speed: Hot Pursuit

notes: Usually hangs upon exiting when played in Big Picture mode. No issues during play. Multiplayer not tested since EA shut the servers down. Picks up the Dualshock 4 nicely (showing XBOX controls).

notes: Lower frame-rate compared to running on Windows
